_source: Gen 2 Compendiums/MEASUREMENT.pdf (Set 3 Trigonometry), TASC MTG215123 Module 3 Topic 3. Angles in degrees. Answer keys verified by tools/verify_answers.py.

unit.title: Trigonometry
unit.subtitle: Module 3 · Topic 3
unit.description: Right-angled trigonometry: Pythagoras' theorem, the trigonometric ratios to find sides and angles, the area rule ½ab·sinC, and applications involving angles of elevation and depression and bearings. Criteria C8 and C1.

lessons.title: Pythagoras' theorem
lessons.overview: Relating the three sides of a right-angled triangle.

sections.title: The theorem
sections.html:
Pythagoras' theorem: in a right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse (the longest side, opposite the right angle) equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides:

c² = a² + b²

sections.title: Finding a side
sections.html: To find the hypotenuse, add the squares and take the square root: legs 6 and 8 give c = √(36 + 64) = √100 = 10. To find a shorter side, subtract: with hypotenuse 13 and one leg 5, the other leg is √(169 − 25) = √144 = 12.

lessons.title: The trigonometric ratios
lessons.overview: Using sine, cosine and tangent to find sides and angles.

sections.title: SOH CAH TOA
sections.html:
Label the sides relative to a known angle: the hypotenuse (opposite the right angle), the opposite (across from the angle) and the adjacent (beside the angle). The three ratios are:

  • sinθ = opposite ÷ hypotenuse (SOH)
  • cosθ = adjacent ÷ hypotenuse (CAH)
  • tanθ = opposite ÷ adjacent (TOA)

sections.title: Finding a side and finding an angle
sections.html:
Side: with a 30° angle and hypotenuse 10, the opposite side is 10×sin30° = 5.

Angle: if the opposite is 5 and the hypotenuse is 10, then sinθ = 0.5, so θ = sin⁻¹(0.5) = 30°. Make sure your calculator is in degrees mode.

lessons.title: Applications: elevation, depression, bearings & area
lessons.overview: Using trigonometry to solve practical problems.

sections.title: Elevation and depression
sections.html: An angle of elevation is measured upward from the horizontal to an object above; an angle of depression is measured downward from the horizontal to an object below. Both create right-angled triangles you can solve with the trig ratios.

sections.title: Bearings
sections.html: A true bearing is measured clockwise from north, written with three digits (e.g. 075°, 240°). Bearings turn navigation problems into right-angled (or general) triangles.

sections.title: Area of a triangle (two sides and the included angle)
sections.html:
When you know two sides and the angle between them, the area is:

Area = ½ × a × b × sin C

For a = 6, b = 8 and C = 30°: ½×6×8×sin30° = 24×0.5 = 12.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Pythagoras & finding sides
quizzes.overview: Pythagoras' theorem and finding sides with the trig ratios. Calculator in degrees mode. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: A right-angled triangle has legs 6 and 8. Find the hypotenuse.
A right-angled triangle has hypotenuse 13 and one leg 5. Find the other leg.
In a right-angled triangle the angle is 30° and the hypotenuse is 10. Find the side OPPOSITE the angle.
In a right-angled triangle the angle is 60° and the hypotenuse is 20. Find the side ADJACENT to the angle.
In a right-angled triangle the angle is 45° and the ADJACENT side is 10. Find the OPPOSITE side.
Which trig ratio equals opposite ÷ hypotenuse? (Type: sin, cos, or tan)
In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is:

choices: The side opposite the right angle
The side adjacent to the right angle

questions.explain: The hypotenuse is the longest side, opposite the right angle.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Finding angles & applications
quizzes.overview: Finding angles, the area rule and applications. Calculator in degrees mode. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: In a right-angled triangle the opposite side is 5 and the hypotenuse is 10. Find the angle (degrees).
In a right-angled triangle the opposite and adjacent sides are both 3. Find the angle (degrees).
In a right-angled triangle the adjacent side is 8 and the hypotenuse is 16. Find the angle (degrees).
Find the area of a triangle with sides a = 6 and b = 8 and included angle C = 30°.
A person 50 m from the base of a tower sees the top at an angle of elevation of 40°. Find the tower's height (metres, 2 d.p.).
The angle of depression from the top of a cliff down to a boat is measured:
questions.explain: An angle of depression is measured downward from the horizontal to an object below.
True bearings are measured clockwise from north, written with three digits.
